summary: Emotional Lovelight‑Stella sandwich, stellar He’s Gone jam
blurb: The second set builds to a soaring Turn On Your Lovelight → Stella Blue → Lovelight sequence and an expansive He's Gone > Matilda jam. Jerry delivers heart‑wringing vocals on Stella while the rhythm section drives the space segment. Highlights include Peggy‑O, Queen Jane Approximately and New Speedway Boogie.
review: On March 21, 1994 at Richfield Coliseum the Dead delivered one of their most emotionally resonant shows of the year. The first set rolled solidly through a classic opening with Greatest Story Ever Told into Bertha, but it was the second set that truly lifted the night – a Lovelight → Stella Blue → Lovelight sandwich that reviewers called "heart‑wringing" and "superb," followed by an expansive He's Gone > Matilda jam that melted into a thunderous Drums/Space climax. Jerry’s guitar was crisp and powerful, his vocals on Stella Blue were described as among the best ever, while Bill and Mickey earned shout‑outs for their kinetic work during the space segment. Standout moments also include Peggy‑O, Queen Jane Approximately and the high‑energy New Speedway Boogie, making this a top‑tier 1994 performance that fans still cite as a highlight of the era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of six recordings are marked positive and one mixed; multiple reviewers praise the Lovelight‑Stella sandwich, He's Gone jam, and overall energy, with consistent language like "crisp," "superb," and "emotional." No significant negative consensus appears, just isolated vocal critique, so a strong positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Crisp‑ish matrix mix captures the emotional Stella Blue sandwich and He's Gone jam with clear instrument separation; despite being a composite, it offers the most balanced sound and full set coverage.

key_highlights: Lovelight → Stella Blue → Lovelight sequence delivers an emotional high point.
He's Gone > Matilda jam flows into a powerful Drums/Space segment.
Peggy‑O, Queen Jane Approximately and New Speedway Boogie stand out as tight, energetic performances.
Jerry’s vocals on Stella Blue are described as heart‑wringing and among his best.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers crisp, powerful guitar throughout and heart‑wringing vocals on Stella Blue; several reviewers call his playing "solid" and note he gave it his all.
band_performance.Phil: Bass is solid but occasionally buried in the mix; a signature bar‑chord riff at the start of Greatest Story Ever Told was missed by some listeners.
band_performance.Vince: Receives mixed feedback – praised for creative keyboard textures by some, while others label his contribution as a "minus star" on this night.
band_performance.Mickey: Shines during the space/drum segment; highlighted for driving the improvisational climax after He's Gone.
band_performance.Billy: Earns shout‑outs for the jam following He's Gone and for powering the Drum/Space transition.
